typecheck.command: xcodebuild -scheme NetMonitor-macOS -configuration Debug build && xcodebuild -scheme NetMonitor-iOS -configuration Debug build
typecheck.details: Both macOS and iOS targets BUILD SUCCEEDED with zero errors. Only pre-existing warnings (trailing whitespace, modifier order, etc.)

lint.details: Only warnings (pre-existing: identifier_name, modifier_order, trailing_whitespace, redundant_string_enum_value). Zero errors. No new lint violations from phase2-ios features.

appliedUpdates.description: Added Phase 2 AR Architecture section to .factory/library/architecture.md: AR Session Manager structure, floor/ceiling classification heuristic (y < 0.3), MeshClassification rawValue swap vs ARKit, floor plan generation pipeline steps, AR coordinate transform formula

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: ios-ui-worker skill says 'Wrap AR-dependent code behind protocols for testability' but the Phase 2 worker did not create an ARSessionManagerProtocol. The ViewModel depends directly on the concrete ARSessionManager class. Consider either: (a) strengthening the skill to explicitly require protocols for all service types including AR managers, or (b) relaxing it to note that protocol-wrapping AR managers is recommended but not required when the concrete class can operate in a degraded simulator mode.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: ARScanViewModel.swift stores `private let sessionManager: ARSessionManager` (concrete type). No ARSessionManagerProtocol exists. Tests work around this by using the real manager in simulator mode. Feature phase2-ar-session-mesh-viz.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: ios-ui-worker skill's TDD Red Phase requirement may be too rigid for pure-function pipeline code where the API surface is dictated by the spec. Consider adding a note that TDD is recommended but pure computation pipelines (e.g., image processing, coordinate math) may implement-then-test when the function signatures are spec-driven.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Worker for phase2-floor-plan-generation deviated from TDD: 'Pure-function math code where API surface was clear from spec'. Worker still achieved 50+ tests with comprehensive coverage.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: run-tests skill should document the rsync workaround for syncing code to mac-mini when git HTTPS auth is broken: `rsync -av --exclude '.git' --exclude 'build' --exclude 'DerivedData' ./ mac-mini:~/Projects/NetMonitor-2.0/`
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Workers for phase2-floor-plan-generation and phase2-survey-transition-tracking both deviated from SSH test step due to known mac-mini git HTTPS auth issue. Used rsync as workaround.

rejectedObservations.observation: AR model types (ARDeviceCapability, ARSessionState, etc.) colocated in ARSessionManager.swift rather than separate Models file
rejectedObservations.reason: Not systemic — minor organizational preference. Types are small enums/structs closely tied to the service. No risk of confusion or conflict.

rejectedObservations.observation: Worker deviated from SSH testing due to known mac-mini git HTTPS auth issue
rejectedObservations.reason: Already documented in AGENTS.md Known Pre-Existing Issues section. Workers correctly used local fallback per documented guidance.
